<!-- GENERATED by behaviors/_spec.py:format_markdown() — do not edit by hand. -->
# Shot Manifest — Behavior Template Format

A *behavior* is a `.yaml` file describing a keying recipe (a fade, an audio
clip, …) that the Shot Manifest applies to objects over a shot's time range.
Behavior names are referenced from a mapping's `default_behaviors` and from a
step's detected behaviors.

## Adding your own

Built-in behaviors ship read-only. **Your own** live in your writable templates
folder under `user_config_root()/mayatk/shot_manifest_behaviors/`; a user file
shadows a built-in of the same name. They are discovered automatically and
available everywhere a behavior name is accepted. YAML is used (not JSON) so you
can comment your templates inline.

## Top-level keys

Schema for one behavior template (see ``BEHAVIOR_FORMAT.md``).

| Key | Required | Description | Example |
|---|---|---|---|
| `description` | no | One-line description of what the behavior does. | `"Fade objects in at shot start via visibility."` |
| `kind` | no | Object kinds this applies to (e.g. ["scene"] or ["audio"]). | `["scene"]` |
| `duration` | no | Length in frames, or "from_source" to size to an audio clip. |  |
| `attributes` | no | Keyframe blocks per attribute, split into in/out phases. | `{"visibility": {"in": {"anchor": "start", "offset": 0, "dur…` |
| `verify` | no | How verification checks the authored keys. | `{"mode": "values_in_range"}` |

Each `attributes.<attr>.<phase>` block holds: `anchor` (`start` | `end` | a
float 0–1), `offset` (frames), `duration` (frames), `values` (list keyframed
across the block), and `tangent` (e.g. `linear`, `step`).

Verification (`verify.mode`) is one of: `exact`, `values_in_range`, `audio_clip`.

## Skeleton (a scene fade)

```yaml
{
  "description": "Fade objects in at shot start via visibility.",
  "kind": [
    "scene"
  ],
  "attributes": {
    "visibility": {
      "in": {
        "anchor": "start",
        "offset": 0,
        "duration": 15,
        "values": [
          0.0,
          1.0
        ],
        "tangent": "linear"
      }
    }
  },
  "verify": {
    "mode": "values_in_range"
  }
}
```

(The skeleton is shown as JSON for compactness — YAML accepts the same data, and
your own files may use YAML comments.)
